title: Memory before aesthetics
description: The system preserves auditable memory, source records, and cycle archives before presentation polish.
rationale: Institutional intelligence must be traceable before it can be beautiful.
authored_by: Dr. Codex & Dr. Claude Windows Platform Team

title: Governance before automation
description: Automated analysis may inform the CIO, but governance gates and CIO authority remain upstream of any action.
rationale: The system is an intelligence institution, not an order-routing machine.
authored_by: Dr. Codex & Dr. Claude Windows Platform Team

title: CIO-only manual execution
description: No BlueLotus V3 agent, deterministic operator, or daemon may place broker orders.
rationale: Research, disagreement, and action menus support Kian Soh as CIO; execution remains manual.
authored_by: Dr. Codex & Dr. Claude Windows Platform Team

title: Protect V2 as source of record
description: BlueLotus3 experimentation must not mutate the protected BlueLotus2 architecture, database, or runtime.
rationale: V3 is an experimental branch; V2 remains stable for the 90-day observation window.
authored_by: Dr. Codex & Dr. Claude Windows Platform Team

title: Auditability before speed
description: Every material cycle, verdict pack, operator result, and agent briefing should be reconstructable from files or database records.
rationale: An institutional pipeline must explain how it reached its conclusions.
authored_by: Dr. Codex & Dr. Claude Windows Platform Team

title: No options as investment instruments
description: Options and derivative instruments are excluded from BlueLotus by deliberate design. BlueLotus invests in underlying businesses and assets only. Options flow data from Moomoo OpenD may be read as a secondary market sentiment signal, but options shall never be held, sized, or recommended as positions.
rationale: Options are a secondary market betting vehicle, not a primary investment vehicle. They are subject to time decay (theta) and expiration, meaning they can destroy value even when the underlying thesis is correct. A thesis-driven, conviction-based portfolio invests in the asset itself — not a derivative claim on its price movement within an arbitrary expiry window. This exclusion is a permanent philosophical position, not an architectural omission.
authored_by: Kian Soh (CIO) — recorded by Dr. Claude Code

title: Dual action gate separation: equity vs. hedge
description: De-risk actions from NITE-PEI CKRI route to equity_action_gate only. VXX and VIXY are governed exclusively by hedge_action_gate. No de-risk recommendation from CKRI shall trigger a reduction of hedge positions. No hedge recommendation shall trigger a change in equity positions. The gates are architecturally separate and must never be merged.
rationale: Hedges are the antidote to the risk that CKRI measures. Reducing hedges in response to high CKRI would be self-defeating — it would increase net portfolio risk at exactly the moment risk is highest. The dual gate architecture enforces this separation at the code level, not in the CIO's memory. VXX and VIXY serve a protective function that must survive any de-risk sequence.
authored_by: Dr. Claude Code — Windows Platform Team

title: Geopolitical game theory outputs are expert-model equilibria, not predictions
description: BGTM-V1 geopolitical advisory outputs represent the equilibrium analysis of expert-specified game models, not empirical forecasts. Payoff matrices are expert-initialised with cold-start uncertainty. Dynamic Geo-LRs are blended with tested static NITE-PEI values via alpha = min(1, calibration_n/30), so an uncalibrated game cannot displace a tested static LR. Every Geo-LR is bounded to [0.1, 10.0]; out-of-range values raise a manual review flag. The CIO must exercise independent judgment on all BGTM-V1 outputs, especially during the calibration_n < 30 cold-start period.
rationale: Game-theoretic payoffs are unobservable and estimated. A model that presented its equilibria as predictions would overstate its certainty and invite over-sizing on cold-start games. Blending, bounding, and the manual-judgment requirement keep the model honest about what it knows. The Python engine validated against the MATLAB oracle (matlab/bgtm_validate.m, 13/13) confirms the arithmetic is correct — but correct arithmetic on expert priors is still an expert opinion, not a fact about the world.
authored_by: Dr. Claude Code — Chief Scientist (recorded for CIO Kian Soh)
